homework.title: Fundamental vs Derived Quantities and SI Units
homework.instructions: Review the list below. Identify which quantities are fundamental and derive their SI units based on standard definitions taught in class.
homework.question:
Classify the following physical quantities as either 'Fundamental' or 'Derived'. For each, write its corresponding SI unit symbol.

List: Length, Mass, Time, Force, Area, Velocity, Energy
homework.answerSpace: Create a table with three columns: Quantity Name | Type (Fundamental/Derived) | SI Unit Symbol.
homework.expectedResponse: Length-Fundamental-m; Mass-Fundamental-kg; Time-Fundamental-s; Force-Derived-N; Area-Derived-m²; Velocity-Derived-m/s; Energy-Derived-J

homework.title: Unit Conversion and Dimensional Analysis Basics
learningOutcomeIds: Convert between units of the same dimension
homework.instructions: Perform the following conversions. Show your working steps clearly using dimensional analysis (factor-label method). Ensure significant figures are maintained where applicable.
homework.question:
Convert the following measurements into their SI base units:

a) 72 km/h to m/s
b) 50 cm² to m²
c) 3.6 × 10⁶ seconds to days (approximate)
d) A force of 5 Newtons expressed in terms of kg, m, and s.
homework.answerSpace: Show the conversion factor used for each calculation and write the final answer with correct units.
homework.expectedResponse: a) 20 m/s; b) 0.005 m² or 5×10⁻³ m²; c) ~41.67 days (or exact calculation); d) kg·m/s²

homework.title: Measurement in Daily Life: Precision and Accuracy
learningOutcomeIds: Understand the importance of standard units
homework.instructions: Think about a measurement task you performed at home or school recently (e.g., measuring height, cooking ingredients, timing an activity). Write a short reflection.
homework.question: Describe one instance where precise measurement was important. What instrument would be best suited for this? Explain why using the concept of 'standard units' in your answer.
homework.answerSpace: Write 3-4 sentences describing the scenario, the tool used (or needed), and the reason standardization matters here.
homework.expectedResponse: Student describes a specific activity (e.g., measuring fabric for curtains) and explains that using non-standard units like 'hand spans' leads to errors, whereas SI units ensure consistency.

homework.title: Dimensional Formula Derivation Challenge
learningOutcomeIds: Derive dimensional formulas for derived quantities
homework.instructions: Using the fundamental dimensions M (Mass), L (Length), and T (Time), derive the dimensional formula for Power. Show your logical steps starting from the definition of Work.
homework.question:
Derive the dimensional formula for 'Power'.

Hint: Recall that Power = Work / Time, and Work = Force × Distance.
homework.answerSpace: Write out the derivation step-by-step ending with [M¹ L² T⁻³].
homework.expectedResponse: Force = MLT⁻²; Work = (MLT⁻²) × L = ML²T⁻²; Power = (ML²T⁻²)/T = ML²T⁻³

learningOutcomesCovered: Identify fundamental quantities and their SI units
Convert between different systems of units (SI)
Understand the importance of standardization in measurement
Derive dimensional formulas for basic physical quantities
